title: systemd/generate: change type to notify
body:
Change the type of units generated with --new from "forking" to
"notify".  This brings Podman closer to systemd and opens up
Podman to a number of use cases (see #5572).

Units generated without --new remain with `type=forking`.  I
experimented a bit with adding a `--sdnotify` flag to `podman start` but
it doesn't really work well since we're competing with the default
sdnotify mode set during container creation.

Fixes: #5572
Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

-->

Want to tackle some issues in the edge space.  Mostly polishing the systemd integration and anything related to journald.
Is this ready? I thought the conmon-based SDNotify implementation was still unmerged, and the default OCI runtime implementation has massive issues and should never be used?

> Is this ready? I thought the conmon-based SDNotify implementation was still unmerged, and the default OCI runtime implementation has massive issues and should never be used?

I think it's ready.  With `--sdnotify=conmon`, conmon isn't much involved as Podman sends the main PID and ready messages.
